title: Signature Editor
text: Signature Editor The Signature Editor enables you to define, edit, and compile component signatures for both Uniface and non-Uniface components. When a component signature is compiled, a signature descriptor that contains information about the component is generated. The descriptors are used at runtime to enable Uniface components to call out to non-Uniface components and non-Uniface components to call in to Uniface components. The Signature Editor enables you to identify a component to Uniface without deciding if it will be a Uniface or non-Uniface implementation. Although the component specification must be identified at this stage, the implementation does not. Component signatures can be modeled or implemented as Uniface components and non-Uniface components. One implementation is set as the deployment default. Declaring multiple implementations for a component allows different implementations to be deployed at runtime without regenerating the application. To enable call-out and call-in, both the component specification and the implementation data of each component is maintained at three levels: Component level—for example, the literal component name Operation level—for example, the literal operation name and the mapping of the return type to a data type Parameter level—for example, the parameter type mapping, such as Numeric to Long Related Links Define Signature Use the Signature Editor (Define Signature) to create or edit the signatures for Uniface and non-Uniface components. Signatures A Uniface signature describes the remote interface of a component. The interface enables other components to address the component without knowing anything about the implementation. This allows components of different origin to communicate through their respective interfaces. Call-In Non-Uniface environments can call in to Uniface components. All communication is passed to the Uniface components, making it possible to create and delete instances of the Uniface components, invoke their operations, and pass data to and from the components. Call-Out Uniface components can activate non-Uniface components, a process known as call-out. Define a Component Signature Use the Signature Editor to create or modify component signatures. Signatures of Uniface components are automatically generated during compilation. Signatures of non-Uniface components can be created or imported. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
